rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MOSES SR. VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A. Nelson, Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2017, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing Century Indemnity Company's motion for an order setting aside default and default judgment entered against James A. Nelson Co., Inc. is granted. The December 20, 2013 entry of default and the July 28, 2016 default judgment entered against defendant James A. Nelson Co., Inc. are set aside. There is no opposition to the motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Order On Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2017, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Cyprus Amax Mineral Company's motion to reconsider order on defendant's motion for summary judgment pursuant to C.C.P. § 1008(a) is granted. Plaintiff does not oppose the motion. Plaintiff's sole request is for the Court to defer this motion until the Court of Appeal issues a final ruling on plaintiff's writ petition, which request is denied. Defendant's motion is unopposed and is therefore granted. The November 22, 2016 order denying defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Company's motion for summary judgment is vacated and defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted as this Court's order granting defendant Colgate-Palmolive Company's motion for summary judgment remains. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Bifurcation Of Successor Liability Issue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2017, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Company's motion for bifurcation of successor liability issue is denied as moot given the ruling on defendant's motion for reconsideration. The Court will inform the parties that if the Court were not to vacate its order denying defendant's motion for summary judgment, then it would deny this motion for bifurcation without prejudice to being raised at trial. This is a matter for the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 1008(A) For An Order Granting Reconsideration Of The November 30, 2016 Order On Defendant Colgate-Palmolive Companys Msj Msa, And For A New Order Denying That Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2017, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 5. Plaintiff's motion pursuant to C.C.P. § 1008(a) for an order granting reconsideration of the November 30, 2016 order on defendant Colgate-Palmolive Company's MSJ/MSA, and for a new order denying that motion is denied. Plaintiff did not demonstrate that the deposition testimony of Dr. Ronald Gordon on which she relies constitutes new or different facts within the meaning of section 1008(a) nor did plaintiff provide a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce the evidence at an earlier time. (C.C.P. § 1008(a), New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, Shiffer v. CBS Corp. (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 246.) Plaintiff's request that the Court enter an order sua sponte without relying on section 1008 is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK FELKER VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for January 11, 2017, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 6. Brayton Purcell LLP, Nancy T. Williams' motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Mark Felker is granted. Counsel demonstrated she provided Mr. Felker with proper notice of the motion and no opposition was filed. Hearing required unless counsel informs plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. =(503/GLW)
